>
[Hauling](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1372)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 80

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Hauling_ weapons are adept at moving creatures around the battlefield after a successful attack.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Command;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You succeed at an attack roll to Strike with a weapon with the _hauling_ rune;  **Effect** The target must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or be moved 5 feet in a direction you choose. This is [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</traits>

**Price** 225 gp

</row>

Hauling (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 80

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The DC is 28, and the target is moved up to 10 feet.
name: Hauling

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Implacable](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1373)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 80

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The effects of the implacable rune only activate when an enemy causes the effect to apply._

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto medium or heavy armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This substantial rune makes you difficult to hold back. Whenever you are affected by an effect that lasts until you [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (for instance, from the [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) action or a [tanglefoot bag](/Equipment.aspx?ID=78)), you become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32). You can use the extra action each round only to Step or Escape.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Implacable

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Memory Palace](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1374)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Structure" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=153" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 80

**Price** 4,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk**  when not activated
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _memory palace_ is an elaborate magical structure that safely stores memories for easy access. It appears to be a miniature Taldan villa small enough to fit in the palm of a human hand.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> (10 minutes) envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The miniature grows into a building connected to the [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8) for 1 hour. The _memory palace_ has a 30-foot-square central courtyard surrounded by 12 10-foot-square chambers. Arranged within these chambers are various items referred to as the palace's nodes—statues, tapestries, fountains, and other works of art are common, but a node can take other forms at the GM's discretion. As a 10-minute activity, you can imprint one extended memory onto a node: the contents of a specific simple book, for example, or the events of a recent adventure. When you do, the node changes its shape and appearance to visually represent the memory you have imprinted onto it. If the node was already imprinted with a memory, the old one can be overwritten to repurpose the node.

 Memories you have imprinted on a node can be mentally accessed at any time by a creature holding the memory palace in its miniature form, regardless of who imprinted them. This grants a +2 item bonus to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) checks relating to details specifically occurring within the memory. What exactly qualifies is up to the GM's discretion, but a node shouldn't grant the benefits for most uses of a given skill. For example, a node dedicated to a book about trees found in the Mwangi Expanse would help with Recall Knowledge checks for such trees, but it wouldn't grant the benefits for all [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) checks to Recall Knowledge.

 A _memory palace_ node can also store the text from a [spellbook](/Equipment.aspx?ID=53) or magical tome, but these memories aren't a substitute for having the book in your possession. A wizard can't prepare spells from a memory palace, though they could use it as source material to try to [Learn a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=25). However, since storing spells in a node would only benefit someone else who later discovers the _memory palace_, few choose to take such an action. The memories contained within the nodes aren't real, and thus don't cause harmful effects, even if the source of the memory was a mental danger. Many _memory palaces_ have leftover memories from previous users—now long dead—imprinted on their various nodes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Memory Palace

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sarkorian God-Caller Garb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1375)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 80

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Explorer's Clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=2)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Each _Sarkorian god-caller garb_ is emblazoned with a unique sigil of a particular Sarkorian deity and adorned with symbols sacred to Sarkorian god callers, the summoners and religious leaders of Sarkoris. These _+1 [explorer's clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=2)_ outfits look as if they were stitched together from multiple types of animals, featuring feathers, fur, leather, and scales.

 When you cast a 3-action summoning spell while wearing this armor, the summoned creature gains a magical sigil that matches the one on the armor. As long as it is within 30 feet of you, the summoned creature gains a +1 status bonus to AC. If it ever moves farther than 30 feet from you, the sigil fades and the creature loses the status bonus to AC, even if it later comes within 30 feet of you again.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sarkorian God-Caller Garb

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sky Serpent Bolt](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1376)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 81

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** bolt

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action or Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This azure bolt is carved in the shape of an undulating snake, its bared fangs framing the point of its head. When an activated _sky serpent bolt_ successfully hits a target, the bolt takes the form of a snake made of pure lightning, dealing 2d12 electricity damage to all creatures in a 30-foot line (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) starting from the target.

 If you spent 2 actions to activate the _sky serpent bolt_, then the line is 60 feet long, and at a single point in the line, you can change the remainder of the line's path by up to a 90-degree angle.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sky Serpent Bolt

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Wand of Fey Flames](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1377)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 81

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This red maple wand carved into a tongue of flame feels warm in your hand.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_faerie fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=107). For the duration of the spell, you can use the limning flames as a source for your magic. Creatures affected by your _faerie fire_ must succeed at a Will save against your spell DC or take a –2 status penalty to their Will saves against [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) and [illusions](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) spells as long as they are affected by _faerie fire_. You can cast an enchantment or illusion spell on such a creature up to a distance twice the spell's normal range. If the spell has a range of touch, or doesn't have a range, it is unaffected by the increase in range from the _faerie fire_.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _faerie fire_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wand of Fey Flames

    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Alluring Scarf](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1379)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 84

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This thin, multicolored scarf shifts between hues in almost dizzying patterns.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You Stride. All creatures within 10 feet of you when you started the Stride must attempt a DC 24 Will save. On a failure, a creature becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) and must spend at least one of its actions on its next turn to move toward you. A fascinated creature can attempt another Will save if you move more than 30 feet away from them, and as normal, acting hostile to a creature or its allies breaks fascination automatically. Creatures that critically failed their save don't receive further saves if you move more than 30 feet away, and acting [hostile](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) to such a creature's allies allows them to attempt another save, rather than automatically ending the fascination. You can Sustain the Activation for up to 1 minute.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Harmonic Hauberk](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1380)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 84

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Chain Shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Rose gold and copper rings in this _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [chain shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6)_ form a vague bird shape. The jingling links tinkle musically, almost like birdsong. While you wear the armor, effects with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait must first [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the _harmonic hauberk_ or they have no effect on you.

 The _harmonic hauberk_ attempts to counteract [_silence_](/Spells.aspx?ID=287) spells cast on you and 4th-level or higher heightened _silence_ spells the first time the armor enters their area (counteract level 4, counteract modifier +20). After failing to counteract a _silence_ spell, the armor can't attempt to counteract the same spell again, even if you leave and enter the spell's area multiple times.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You have a pool of Focus Points and at least one [composition](/Traits.aspx?ID=31) spell; **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point and immediately use the Focus Point to cast [_lingering composition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=389), using your [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) check to determine the effects of the spell, followed by the [_inspire defense_](/Spells.aspx?ID=387) composition cantrip. You can use this activation to cast those spells even if you don't have the [Lingering Composition](/Feats.aspx?ID=180) or [Inspire Defense](/Feats.aspx?ID=188) feats.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Harmonic Hauberk

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 84 **Price** 10,000 gp **Bulk** L when not activated --- This item appears to be a miniature stone replica of an aristocratic home or a simple castle. **Activate** (10 minutes) command, envision, Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You place the figurine on the ground, and a harmonious note rings out as it grows into a spacious, elegant ballroom. The ballroom is 60 feet long, 45 feet wide, and features a ceiling that rises to a height of 20 feet. Elegant double doors on either end of the ballroom allow entry. Inside, the ballroom is fully stocked with tables and chairs along both sides. A feasting table in the center of each seating area provides enough food and drink for up to 36 guests, and a cadre of [_unseen servants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=352) tend to guests' basic needs.
While inside, you can utter a command word to call forth music, which echoes through the ballroom. Repeating the command word can alter the dynamics of the music, such as the musical style or tempo, or stop the music entirely. A second command word produces a set of masked, illusory dancers that immediately take to the floor and begin dancing to the current music. When there's no music, the illusory guests chatter among themselves in a nonsense language. An illusory dancer will gladly dance with anyone who asks. Repeating the command word dismisses the dancers. You can utter a third command word declaring the gala over to revert the ballroom to its original form. As it reverts, the illusory dancers clap and cheer for your hospitality. If you don't revert the ballroom on your own, it automatically returns to its original state at the next sunrise.
name: Pocket Gala

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 85 **Price** 14,000 gp **Usage** worn armor **Bulk** 1 **Base Armor** [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10) --- With its standard-grade mithral polished to a mirrorlike sheen, this _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [glamered](/Equipment.aspx?ID=286) [mithral](/Equipment.aspx?ID=144) [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10)_ features the religious symbols of the goddesses of [the Prismatic Ray](/Deities.aspx?ID=207) pantheon—[Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=5), [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=16), and [Shelyn](/Deities.aspx?ID=17)—surrounded by a rainbow-colored set of gems. **Activate** command, Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You surround yourself in a coruscating field of red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet light for 1 minute. Each color has a different effect, based on the effects of a 7th-level [_chromatic wall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=37) spell, and you apply the effects of each color once before it disappears from your field of light, which can happen in the following two ways.
First, the field attempts a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check (counteract level 7, counteract modifier +27) against any effect that would be blocked by a _chromatic wall_ of any of the colors still surrounding you in your field of light. Succeed or fail, that color then disappears from your field of light.
Second, any creature that touches you or damages you with an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack or non-[reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) melee weapon is affected by a random remaining color from your field. As normal, this doesn't affect creatures you choose to touch.
A creature can also remove a color from your field of light by using a specific spell, as described in _chromatic wall_. The effect ends early if each color disappears from your field of light, or if you choose to Dismiss it. **Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include 1,600 gp of mithral.
name: Prismatic Plate

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Good" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=83" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 85

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Spiked Chain](/Weapons.aspx?ID=60)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [disrupting](/Equipment.aspx?ID=294) [spiked chain](/Weapons.aspx?ID=60)_ comes in a rich, green color with rose petals painted on the handle in varying shades of red and pink. The chain seems to banish nearby shadows.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You hold the chain out before you, and the weapon glows as bright as a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60). You can suppress this light by Activating the weapon again.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You critically succeed at a Strike against a [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160);  **Effect** The weapon unleashes its light in an attempt to destroy the target. The _vine of roses_ casts a 3rd-level [_searing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=269) spell with the triggering creature as the target. In place of a spell attack roll, make a weapon attack roll with the _vine of roses_ using the same multiple attack penalty as the triggering Strike.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vine of Roses

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Thundering Echoes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1385)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=147" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 85

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A forked, lightning-like crack runs down the length of this ornate stone wand, which rumbles slightly with the peals of distant thunder.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_sound burst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) of the indicated level. After you Cast the Spell, at the start of each of your turns, the sound echoes in the same area as if you had cast it again, though it deals one fewer d10. This effect lasts until the damage is reduced to below 2d10. The echoes don't affect structures or other items.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _sound burst_ of the appropriate level.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _sound burst_ of the appropriate level.

</traits>

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

With this rune, you can instantly retrieve an item in your possession without digging around looking for it.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** You have a free hand and the _called_ item is in a bag, pack, pouch, or other container on your person, or unattended within 30 feet; **Effect** The item teleports to a free hand.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Called (Accessory Rune)

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision ([metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107))   **Requirements** You're receiving a bonus to AC from your _dragon's breath_ cape or shield; **Effect** If your next action is to Cast a Spell with an area of effect that deals the same type of damage as the depicted dragon's breath weapon, the spell gains the effects of the [Widen Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=315) feat. The rune can only affect spells of a specific level or lower, determined by the type of rune.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Paired](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1388)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 88

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** each rune applied to a separate item that has pockets

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These runes always come in pairs and can be applied to a garment's pockets to be activated.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** The _paired_ items are both invested, typically by two different characters, and are within 100 feet of each other; **Effect** Items in the pockets (up to 10 negligible Bulk items or 1 light Bulk item per pocket) trade places via teleportation.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Paired
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 88

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Paired (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 88

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You can activate these runes at a distance of up to 1 mile.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Paired (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 88

**Price** 2,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You can activate these runes at any distance as long as they're on the same plane.
name: Paired

If you roll a success, you can Grab the Edge even if you don't have a hand free.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spell-Bastion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1392)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 89

**Price** 2,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _spell-bastion_ rune creates a reservoir of eldritch energy within the shield. A spellcaster can spend 1 minute to Cast a Spell of 3rd level or lower into the shield. The spell must take 2 actions or fewer to cast and must be able to target a creature other than the caster. The spell has no immediate effect—it's instead stored for later. When you invest a _spell-bastion_ shield, you immediately know the name and level of the stored spell. A _spell-bastion_ shield found as treasure has a 50% chance of having a spell of the GM's choice stored in it.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;    **Requirements** Your _spell-bastion_ shield is storing a spell, and you have your shield raised; **Effect** You prepare to unleash the stored spell on any foe that hits you until your next turn.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> command;   **Trigger** You use the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) reaction against a foe that the stored spell can target; **Requirements** You have a spell stored in your _spell-bastion_ shield, and you've activated the shield since your last turn in preparation for unleashing the spell; **Effect** You unleash the stored spell, which uses the target of the triggering attack as the target of the spell. This empties the spell from the shield and allows a spell to be cast into it again.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** You harmlessly expend the stored spell. This frees the shield to have a new spell cast into it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spell-Bastion

---

This weighted animal bladder can be inflated with air in preparation for a dive. It holds enough air to breathe for one round. As a free action, you can inhale the contents of the air bladder, which resets the number of rounds you can hold your breath (see the rules for [drowning and suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468)). You can inflate the bladder or remove its attached weight as an Interact action. An unattached inflated bladder without the weight will float toward the surface of the water at a rate of 60 feet per round.

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 92

**Price** 1 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn under light armor or clothes

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Adventurers have found a number of uses for these animal blood–filled bladders, which were originally used in theatrical productions. Whenever you take slashing or piercing damage with the fake blood pack under your clothes or armor, roll a DC 11 flat check. On a success, the blood pack is punctured. You or an ally can puncture the hidden pack intentionally. When faking an injury, the blood pack grants a +2 item bonus to relevant [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks, such as to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) about being injured. Abilities that trigger when a creature deals bleed damage, determine if a creature is bleeding, or are otherwise based on bleed damage don't trigger or apply for blood from a fake blood pack, which might mean creatures with such abilities automatically realize the ruse.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fake Blood Pack

The same replacement filters used in [water purifiers](/Equipment.aspx?ID=877) can be used with a plague mask, granting you the counteract modifier and effects of the filter for 20 minutes. Plague masks are uncommon items because the filters they use to protect from inhaled poisons and diseases are themselves uncommon. As such, you can buy a plague mask without a filter as a common item, though it's usually more cost-effective to buy a fine mask in the shape of a plague mask in that case.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Powder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1399)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 92

**Price** 1 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 1

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A bag of powder contains powdered chalk, flour, or similar materials. In addition to other uses for powder, it can be handy while adventuring to help pinpoint invisible creatures. You can throw the powder into an adjacent square as an Interact action. If there's a creature in that square, it becomes temporarily [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=27) until the end of your turn, though the creature still has [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) due to invisibility. The powder quickly falls away or becomes invisible itself, preventing you from tracking the creature indefinitely.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Powder

---

These goggles use lenses made out of smoked glass to protect against creatures with gaze attacks. While wearing smoked goggles, you're always considered to be [Averting your Gaze](/Actions.aspx?ID=92), but all creatures have [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from you. The fact that the goggles conceal creatures is part of what grants the wearer the item's benefits. If you have a way to negate the concealment from the smoked goggles, you no longer gain the benefit, either.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Smoked Goggles

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Swarmsuit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1401)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 93, [Pathfinder #201: Pactbreaker](/Sources.aspx?ID=241) pg. 46

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to  explorer's clothing 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These thick, overlapping layers of clothing are coupled with a matching hat, outfitted with mesh netting around its wide brim to keep you safe from insects. You gain resistance 3 to physical damage from [swarms](/Traits.aspx?ID=239). Explorer's clothing altered in this way has a Dexterity cap of +2, check penalty of –1, and Speed penalty of –5 feet regardless of your Strength.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

---

An impenetrable swarmsuit is made of expensive, finely crafted mesh designed to completely prevent swarms from getting inside. You gain resistance 10 to physical damage from swarms while wearing an impenetrable swarmsuit, rather than 3.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tear-Away Clothing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1402)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 93

**Price** 5 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** modifies existing clothing

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Performers and criminals are both known to use disposable clothing designed to be torn off the body quickly and easily. This garment is loose enough to be worn over another outfit, including light armor. You can remove tear-away clothing with an Interact action. The price for tear-away clothing is to modify an existing outfit. If purchasing a new outfit, add the tear-away clothing's price to the outfit to modify it as part of the purchase.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tear-Away Clothing

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Waffle Iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1403)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 93

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This set of hinged metal plates features studs on the inside of each plate to provide a texture for the pastry cakes you cook with it. You pour batter on the plates, close the device, and place it on a fire or stove to cook.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Waffle Iron
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 93

**Price** 5 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Waffle Iron (Imprint)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 93

**Price** 2 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The plates of this waffle iron feature a noble insignia, a religious symbol, or other design to imprint onto the pastry.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Waffle Iron (Mithral)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 93

**Price** 355 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The plates of this waffle iron are made of [standard-grade mithral](/Equipment.aspx?ID=275) rather than iron and might bear an imprint on the plates. As with other mithral cookware, food rarely sticks to a mithral waffle iron. As it's made of mithral, this is an uncommon item.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Waffle Iron (High-Grade Mithral)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 93

**Price** 6,005 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

Truly fit for emperors and demigods, this waffle iron is made of high-grade mithral. Although most metallurgists are certain that the much more affordable standard-grade mithral waffle iron maintains mithral's full non-stick properties, the super elite insist that only waffles made from high-grade mithral waffle irons conform to the most perfect shape and texture. Nadian has one high-grade mithral waffle iron for sale emblazoned with an archaic symbol associated with [Milani](/Deities.aspx?ID=43) from before she became a deity, supposedly used to make Milani herself waffles during her mortal life. As it's made of mithral, this is an uncommon item.
name: Waffle Iron

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This set of hinged metal plates features studs on the inside of each plate to provide a texture for the pastry cakes you cook with it. You pour batter on the plates, close the device, and place it on a fire or stove to cook.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Waffle Iron
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 93

**Price** 5 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Waffle Iron (Imprint)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 93

**Price** 2 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

---

The plates of this waffle iron feature a noble insignia, a religious symbol, or other design to imprint onto the pastry.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Waffle Iron (Mithral)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 93

**Price** 355 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The plates of this waffle iron are made of [standard-grade mithral](/Equipment.aspx?ID=275) rather than iron and might bear an imprint on the plates. As with other mithral cookware, food rarely sticks to a mithral waffle iron. As it's made of mithral, this is an uncommon item.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Waffle Iron (High-Grade Mithral)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 93

**Price** 6,005 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

Truly fit for emperors and demigods, this waffle iron is made of high-grade mithral. Although most metallurgists are certain that the much more affordable standard-grade mithral waffle iron maintains mithral's full non-stick properties, the super elite insist that only waffles made from high-grade mithral waffle irons conform to the most perfect shape and texture. Nadian has one high-grade mithral waffle iron for sale emblazoned with an archaic symbol associated with [Milani](/Deities.aspx?ID=43) from before she became a deity, supposedly used to make Milani herself waffles during her mortal life. As it's made of mithral, this is an uncommon item.
name: Waffle Iron (High-Grade Mithral)

>
[Wheelbarrow](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1404)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 93

**Price** 5 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 5
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small, hand-propelled vehicle has a single wheel and is designed to carry large loads over a distance. A wheelbarrow can typically hold up to 5 Bulk of objects without issue. The GM might rule that it can hold more Bulk of particular items, such as sand, or less Bulk of other items, like awkwardly shaped rocks.

 You can raise or place a wheelbarrow using an Interact action and can Stride your normal Speed while you have the wheelbarrow raised, though you're [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=12) while pushing a wheelbarrow. You can Release a wheelbarrow as normal, but a loaded wheelbarrow has a chance of tipping over if you don't place it down with care. When you Release a wheelbarrow, attempt a DC 7 flat check. On a failure, the wheelbarrow tips over, spilling its contents into a randomly determined adjacent space.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wheelbarrow

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 96 --- A blue-green metal with an eerie green luminescence, abysium radiates power that's inimical to life. Careless exposure to the material can lead to long-term damage to the immune system; as such, mining abysium is hazardous, as large quantities of the metal in an area cause all nearby creatures to become sick. A creature carrying an abysium object is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) for a standard-grade object of light Bulk, sickened 2 for a standard-grade object of 1 Bulk or more or a high-grade object of light Bulk, or sickened 3 for a high-grade object of 1 Bulk or more. This and all other sickening effects of abysium are [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) effects. Crafters can use 1 abysium chunk to create up to 6 doses of poisonous [abysium powder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1407). Unscrupulous smiths have harnessed abysium's toxic properties to create noxious weapons and deadly substances. All objects crafted from abysium shed dim light in a 10-foot radius. ## Abysium

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Abysium Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1406)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 96

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk**  varies by armor
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Abysium](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1405)

</column>

---

Typically only creatures immune to abysium's effects would don abysium armor. You're [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) while wearing standard-grade armor made from abysium, or sickened 3 while wearing high-grade armor made from abysium. You can't reduce your sickened condition while wearing abysium armor, or for 1 hour after removing it. Abysium armor is dangerous to nearby creatures, too. Creatures within 10 feet of abysium armor must succeed at a Fortitude save (DC 30 for standard grade or DC 40 for high grade) or become sickened 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure). Afterwards, the creature is temporarily immune for 1 minute, but if they remain within the area for longer than 1 minute, they automatically critically fail the next save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 12"

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

</title>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include at least 20,000 gp of abysium + 2,000 gp per Bulk.

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Contact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=37" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 96

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This faintly glowing powder rapidly induces the symptoms of abysium poisoning.

 **Saving Throw** DC 27 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 6 minutes; **Stage 1** 8d6 poison damage and [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (1 minute); **Stage 2** 9d6 poison damage and sickened 2 (1 minute); **Stage 3** 10d6 poison damage and sickened 3 (1 minute)

**Craft Requirements** [abysium](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1405) worth at least 75 gp.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Abysium Powder

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Abysium Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1408)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 96

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Abysium](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1405)

</column>

---

These glowing shields are crafted to ensure that the abysium used is safely contained within a durable outer shell. If the shield breaks, however, the toxic metal becomes exposed. When you use the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) reaction against an adjacent creature's melee Strike, if the shield breaks, the creature is exposed to the abysium and is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) for a standard-grade shield or sickened 2 for a high-grade shield.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Abysium Weapon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1409)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 97

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by weapon

**Bulk**  varies by weapon
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Abysium](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1405)

</column>

---

Abysium weapons are safe to carry, as the toxic metal is contained within an outer shell. However, the inherent toxicity in these blue-green weapons can irradiate open wounds and poison foes. Abysium weapons have one fewer property rune slot, but they deal 1d4 poison damage on a successful Strike, and on a critical hit, the target is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), or sickened 2 with high-grade abysium.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<column gap="tiny">

</row>

**Base Material** [Abysium](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1405)

</column>

---

Abysium weapons are safe to carry, as the toxic metal is contained within an outer shell. However, the inherent toxicity in these blue-green weapons can irradiate open wounds and poison foes. Abysium weapons have one fewer property rune slot, but they deal 1d4 poison damage on a successful Strike, and on a critical hit, the target is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), or sickened 2 with high-grade abysium.

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 97 --- This rust red metal is liquid at room temperature, making it challenging for all but the most skilled metallurgists to craft with and earning it the name “quickiron” in some places. Djezet is also extremely reactive to magic, even in its solid, workable alloyed form. It glows when targeted by magic, and objects crafted with djezet alloys glow with scarlet striations, which lead some smiths to nickname it “tiger iron.” When targeted by spells, objects crafted from djezet alloys exhibit these glowing red markings that last for 1 round or the duration of the spell, whichever is longer. A djezet mass contains enough djezet to refine into up to two [djezet doses](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1412). ## Djezet Alloy

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Djezet Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1411)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 98

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk**  varies by armor
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Djezet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1410)

</column>

---

Armor made of djezet alloy is easy to enchant. Transferring runes onto djezet armor from other armor waives the usual 1/10 cost to etch the rune. This benefit has no effect on the cost of the rune itself or on the cost to transfer a rune off djezet armor onto a non-djezet armor. High-grade djezet armor can be etched with an additional property rune (to a maximum of four instead of three for _+3 armor_).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 1,800 gp (+180 gp per Bulk)

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include at least 225 gp of djezet + 22.5 gp per Bulk.

level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Standard"
>
Djezet Armor (High-Grade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include at least 17,500 gp of djezet + 1,750 gp per Bulk.
name: Djezet Armor

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Djezet Dose](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1412)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 98

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Some mages carry vials of [djezet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1410) for its magic-enhancing properties. When you drink a djezet dose, if your next action is to Cast a Spell, you can apply the effects of the [Reach Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=181) or [Widen Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=315) metamagic feats.

**Craft Requirements** At least 300 gp of djezet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Djezet Dose

When taking energy damage from a spell, the shield's listed Hardness doubles.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Djezet Weapon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1414)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 98

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by weapon

**Bulk**  varies by weapon
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Djezet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1410)

</column>

---

The djezet in weapons absorbs magical power. Critical hits made with a djezet weapon against a prepared or spontaneous spellcaster cause the target to lose one prepared spell or one spontaneous spell slot unless the target succeeds at a Will save (DC 30 for standard-grade or DC 40 for high-grade). The spell is randomly selected from among the caster's highest three spell levels (and then from among the spells prepared in that level, for a prepared spellcaster).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Djezet Weapon (Standard-Grade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 98

**Price** 1,800 gp (+180 gp per Bulk)

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** at least 225 gp of djezet + 22.5 gp per Bulk

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Djezet Weapon (High-Grade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 98

**Price** 22,000 gp (+2,200 gp per Bulk)

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** at least 11,000 gp of djezet + 1,100 gp per Bulk
name: Djezet Weapon

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Inubrix Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1416)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 98

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk**  varies by armor
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Inubrix](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1415)

</column>

---

Inubrix's malleable nature means armor crafted from it is less rigid than usual, though this slightly increased mobility comes at the cost of protection. Metal armor crafted from inubrix gains the [flexible](/Traits.aspx?ID=204) armor trait; its item bonus to AC decreases by 1 but its maximum Dexterity bonus increases by 1.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 1,200 gp (+120 gp per Bulk)

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include at least 150 gp of inubrix + 15 gp per Bulk.

level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Inubrix Armor (High-Grade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 98

**Price** 18,000 gp (+1,800 gp per Bulk)

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include at least 9,000 gp of inubrix + 900 gp per Bulk.
name: Inubrix Armor

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Inubrix Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1417)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 99

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Inubrix](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1415)

</column>

---

Inubrix is much softer than steel, and it's atypical for it to be used to make shields. An item made by an unskilled smith will dent from a single blow and prove worthless on the battlefield. However, its yielding surface can entrap metal weapons. You can use an inubrix shield (or [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=41) or [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=42) on an inubrix shield) to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) a metal item with the [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) skill even if you don't have a free hand; doing so uses the weapon's reach (if different from your own) and adds the weapon's item bonus to attack rolls (if any) as an item bonus to the Athletics check. If you critically fail a check to Disarm a metal item using the weapon, you can drop the weapon to take the effects of a failure instead of a critical failure. On a critical success, you still need a free hand if you want to take the item.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

[Inubrix Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1417)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 99

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Inubrix](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1415)

</column>

---

Inubrix is much softer than steel, and it's atypical for it to be used to make shields. An item made by an unskilled smith will dent from a single blow and prove worthless on the battlefield. However, its yielding surface can entrap metal weapons. You can use an inubrix shield (or [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=41) or [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=42) on an inubrix shield) to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) a metal item with the [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) skill even if you don't have a free hand; doing so uses the weapon's reach (if different from your own) and adds the weapon's item bonus to attack rolls (if any) as an item bonus to the Athletics check. If you critically fail a check to Disarm a metal item using the weapon, you can drop the weapon to take the effects of a failure instead of a critical failure. On a critical success, you still need a free hand if you want to take the item.

    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Inubrix Weapon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1418)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 99

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by weapon

**Bulk**  varies by weapon
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Inubrix](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1415)

</column>

---

While inubrix weapons don't pack the same punch as more durable weapons, they have the unique ability to bypass some of the protections offered by metal armors and shields. A weapon crafted from inubrix reduces the weapon's damage die by 1 size. However, they ignore the resistance to damage from metal armor's armor specialization effects and the circumstance bonus to AC from metal shields. Strikes with inubrix weapons don't trigger the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) reaction from a metal shield. Weapons that normally deal 1d4 damage can't be crafted from inubrix.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 1,400 gp (+140 gp per Bulk)

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** at least 175 gp of inubrix + 17.5 gp per Bulk

level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Inubrix Weapon (High-Grade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 13,500 gp (+1,350 gp per Bulk)

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** at least 6,750 gp of inubrix + 675 gp per Bulk

markdown:
**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 99 --- Light and strong, noqual also demonstrates a powerful resistance to magic. A side effect of this resistance is that making magical weapons out of noqual requires complex and expensive alchemical treatments. Kevoth-Kul, the Black Sovereign of Numeria, has developed an alloy of noqual and cold iron known as [sovereign steel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=776) to help mitigate this property. The metal's crystalline appearance might suggest that it's fragile, but the pale-green material can be worked similarly to iron. Objects made of noqual have a +4 circumstance bonus on saves against magic that the item attempts and grant their bonus to saves the owner makes specifically to protect the item from magic (such as against the [_rusting grasp_](/Spells.aspx?ID=711) spell). ## Noqual

markdown:
**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 99 --- Light and strong, noqual also demonstrates a powerful resistance to magic. A side effect of this resistance is that making magical weapons out of noqual requires complex and expensive alchemical treatments. Kevoth-Kul, the Black Sovereign of Numeria, has developed an alloy of noqual and cold iron known as [sovereign steel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=776) to help mitigate this property. The metal's crystalline appearance might suggest that it's fragile, but the pale-green material can be worked similarly to iron. Objects made of noqual have a +4 circumstance bonus on saves against magic that the item attempts and grant their bonus to saves the owner makes specifically to protect the item from magic (such as against the [_rusting grasp_](/Spells.aspx?ID=711) spell). ## Noqual

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Noqual Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1420)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 100

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk**  varies by armor
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Noqual](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1419)

</column>

---

The mere sight of a suit of noqual armor is enough to make some opposing spellcasters withdraw from the battlefield. While wearing noqual armor, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC against spell attack rolls. If you Cast a Spell while wearing noqual armor, you must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or the spell fails.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 12"

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 1,600 gp (+160 gp per Bulk)

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include at least 200 gp of noqual + 20 gp per Bulk.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Standard"
>
Noqual Armor (High-Grade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 100

**Price** 32,000 gp (+3,200 gp per Bulk)

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include at least 16,000 gp of noqual + 1,600 gp per Bulk.
name: Noqual Armor

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Noqual Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1421)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 100

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Noqual](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1419)

</column>

---

Noqual shields are notoriously difficult to make, requiring high-grade materials, but they're especially effective against magic. When you have a noqual shield [Raised](/Actions.aspx?ID=98), you gain its circumstance bonus to saving throws against spells that target you (as well as AC). Additionally, while the shield is Raised, when an opponent casting a spell that targets you critically fails a spell attack roll against your AC, you can reflect the spell back toward them as a reaction. Attempt a ranged attack roll against the triggering creature using your highest proficiency with a ranged weapon. If you succeed, your opponent takes the effects of a successful spell attack roll for their own spell (or the effects of a critical success if your attack roll was a critical success).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 100

**Price** 14,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The shield has Hardness 7, HP 28, and BT 14.

**Craft Requirements** noqual worth at least 7,000 gp

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

</row>

---

**Craft Requirements** noqual worth at least 7,700 gp

<title

    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Noqual Weapon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1422)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 100

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by weapon

**Bulk**  varies by weapon
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Noqual](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1419)

</column>

---

Noqual weapons are inimical to [summoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=154) creatures and spell effects that can be damaged by conventional attacks. Against such targets, a Strike with a noqual weapon gains a circumstance bonus to damage equal to twice the number of weapon damage dice. In addition, noqual weapons disrupt spellcasters' concentration, causing them to become [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round on a critical hit.

---

Noqual weapons are inimical to [summoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=154) creatures and spell effects that can be damaged by conventional attacks. Against such targets, a Strike with a noqual weapon gains a circumstance bonus to damage equal to twice the number of weapon damage dice. In addition, noqual weapons disrupt spellcasters' concentration, causing them to become [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round on a critical hit.

markdown:
**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 100 --- In its raw state, this silvery ore is either scalding hot or freezing cold. Metallurgists disagree over whether siccatite is two related substances or one substance that determines its temperature output via some unknown process. Whatever the reason, the extreme temperature of the material means it must be handled carefully. Hot siccatite can easily ignite flammables such as paper and dry brush, and cold siccatite left in moist areas quickly surrounds itself with a thick layer of ice. A creature that comes into physical contact with a significant amount of siccatite takes 1 energy damage for each round of continued contact (either fire or cold damage, for hot and cold siccatite respectively). ## Siccatite

</title>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 100

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk**  varies by armor
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Siccatite](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1423)

</column>

---

Siccatite armor must be fitted with protective undercoats for it to be safely donned, making it heavier than other types of armor; add 1 Bulk to the armor's typical weight. When wearing cold siccatite armor, you're protected from [severe and extreme environmental heat](/Rules.aspx?ID=642), and when wearing hot siccatite armor, you're protected from severe and extreme environmental cold. An opponent that has you [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) with its body while you're wearing standard-grade siccatite armor takes 4 energy damage at the end of its turn (either fire or cold, for hot and cold siccatite armor respectively), or 6 damage for a high-grade siccatite armor.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Siccatite Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1425)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 101

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Siccatite](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1423)

</column>

---

Siccatite shields radiate extreme temperatures that protect them from energy damage. A protective coating on the wielder's side of the shield protects them from siccatite's radiant heat or cold but makes the shields bulkier than average. Standard-grade siccatite shields have resistance 10 to the corresponding energy type: fire damage for a hot siccatite shield or cold damage for a cold siccatite shield, as it takes even more extreme temperatures to affect them. They also provide some resistance to opposing temperatures because of their own extreme temperature, which extends to you when you [Raise the Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98). The shield has resistance 5 to the opposing damage type (cold damage for a hot siccatite shield or fire damage for a cold siccatite shield), and while you have a siccatite shield Raised, you gain that resistance to the opposing damage type as well. For a high-grade siccatite shield, the resistances increase from 10 to 20 for damage of the corresponding type, and from 5 to 10 for damage of the opposing type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Siccatite Buckler (Standard-Grade)
</title>

<traits>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Siccatite Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1425)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 101

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Siccatite](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1423)

</column>

---

Siccatite shields radiate extreme temperatures that protect them from energy damage. A protective coating on the wielder's side of the shield protects them from siccatite's radiant heat or cold but makes the shields bulkier than average. Standard-grade siccatite shields have resistance 10 to the corresponding energy type: fire damage for a hot siccatite shield or cold damage for a cold siccatite shield, as it takes even more extreme temperatures to affect them. They also provide some resistance to opposing temperatures because of their own extreme temperature, which extends to you when you [Raise the Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98). The shield has resistance 5 to the opposing damage type (cold damage for a hot siccatite shield or fire damage for a cold siccatite shield), and while you have a siccatite shield Raised, you gain that resistance to the opposing damage type as well. For a high-grade siccatite shield, the resistances increase from 10 to 20 for damage of the corresponding type, and from 5 to 10 for damage of the opposing type.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Siccatite Weapon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1426)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 101

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by weapon

**Bulk**  varies by weapon
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Siccatite](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1423)

</column>

---

Crafting weapons from siccatite is often enough to drive smiths to despair, as the extreme temperature of the metal can cause it to shatter while being worked. Siccatite weapons have substantial grip wrappings to protect their wielders from the metal's extreme temperatures. They're constantly surrounded by a heat haze or a halo of rime, depending on the siccatite's type. Siccatite weapons automatically gain either a [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) or [frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296) property rune, for hot and cold siccatite respectively, even if they aren't otherwise enchanted; this rune can't be removed, and it deals 1d8 damage instead of 1d6 damage. This uses one of the weapon's property rune slots as normal. High-grade siccatite gains a _greater flaming_ (hot siccatite) or _greater frost_ (cold siccatite) property instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</column>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 101

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by weapon

**Bulk**  varies by weapon
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Siccatite](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1423)

</column>

---

Crafting weapons from siccatite is often enough to drive smiths to despair, as the extreme temperature of the metal can cause it to shatter while being worked. Siccatite weapons have substantial grip wrappings to protect their wielders from the metal's extreme temperatures. They're constantly surrounded by a heat haze or a halo of rime, depending on the siccatite's type. Siccatite weapons automatically gain either a [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) or [frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296) property rune, for hot and cold siccatite respectively, even if they aren't otherwise enchanted; this rune can't be removed, and it deals 1d8 damage instead of 1d6 damage. This uses one of the weapon's property rune slots as normal. High-grade siccatite gains a _greater flaming_ (hot siccatite) or _greater frost_ (cold siccatite) property instead.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Burnished Plating](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1427)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 104

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to metal armor

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These highly polished metal plates can be added to any armor. While wearing armor with burnished plating, you gain the Sunshine! reaction. However, you take a –4 circumstance penalty to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks except in darkness, and your armor's Strength entry increases its value by 2, requiring you to have a higher Strength score to overcome the armor's penalties. Even if you meet your armor's new Strength entry, you still take the penalty to Stealth checks. When you are critically hit by an attack that deals bludgeoning damage, burnished plating stops working until someone spends 10 minutes repairing and polishing it; this doesn't require a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check.

 **Sunshine!** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature you can see targets you with an attack; **Requirements** You are in an area of bright light; **Effect** You twist to reflect light in your foe's eyes. The attacking creature must succeed at a DC 3 flat check or the attack fails. The attacker doesn't need to roll this flat check if it has a precise sense other than vision or if it would already need to roll a flat check with a higher DC to target you, such as if you are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) or [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Burnished Plating

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragon's Crest](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1428)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 104

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to a shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This variant on the shield boss is usually shaped like a dragon's head, its jaws open wide enough to wedge a small vessel of liquid within. A dragon's crest can be etched with weapon runes, much like a [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=41) or [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=42), but doesn't otherwise alter your shield's statistics. A shield bearing a dragon's crest can't be combined with an [attached](/Traits.aspx?ID=171) weapon, like shield spikes.

 You can use an Interact action to wedge an [alchemical bomb](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=7) into the crest's jaws. While a bomb is wedged in the shield, you take a –1 penalty to attack rolls with the shield. When you successfully Strike a creature with the shield, the vessel shatters, exposing the creature you hit—and yourself— to the substance within. This counts as a successful hit with the bomb against the creature if it's adjacent to you or a successful hit against the appropriate adjacent square if it's not. The bomb deals [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage as normal to surrounding creatures, including you. In addition, the bomb deals full damage to the attached shield.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragon's Crest

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Reinforced Surcoat](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1429)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 104

**Price** 2 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to medium or heavy armor

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This surcoat is made of thick fabric and light chain, designed to protect vital areas. When you are critically hit by an attack, you gain physical resistance equal to 2 + the value of the armor's potency rune against the attack's damage. If the armor is in the [chain](/ArmorGroups.aspx?ID=1) armor group and you have its armor specialization effect, you instead increase the physical resistance from the chain armor specialization by 2. This can't reduce the damage to less than the damage rolled for the hit before doubling for a critical hit. However, the reinforced surcoat increases the Speed penalty of your armor by 5 feet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Reinforced Surcoat

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shield Augmentation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1430)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 104

**Price** 8 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to a shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

There are numerous methods to modify shields—snarling rods to catch weapons, bladed edges, padding for nonlethal strikes, and so on—but all share basic functionality. A shield augmentation can be etched with weapon runes, much like a [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=41) or [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=42), but doesn't otherwise alter your shield's statistics. A shield bearing an augmentation can't be combined with an [attached](/Traits.aspx?ID=171) weapon, like shield spikes.

 A shield augmentation grants your shield one or more weapon traits, chosen when the augmentation is created. You can either choose to add the [backswing](/Traits.aspx?ID=173) or [forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180) trait, or you can choose two of the following weapon traits: [disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196), or [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shield Augmentation

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Twining Chains](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1431)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 104

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This set of chains is completely covered with spikes and sharp blades. Not balanced for weapon use, twining chains are instead wrapped around a user's body as a deterrent to attackers—and, for some, a fashion statement. While wearing twining chains, you gain the Thorns reaction. In addition to their dangers, twining chains' weight also increases your armor's Bulk by 1, Strength entry value by 2, and check penalty by 2.

 **Thorns** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** You are hit by an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack; **Effect** You twist to harm your attacker with your chains' spikes and blades. Make an attack roll against the triggering attacker using your unarmed attack modifier. On a hit, the attacking creature takes 1d6 piercing damage. This damage increases to 2d6 damage if the armor has a _[+1 potency_ rune](/Equipment.aspx?ID=278), 3d6 damage if the armor has a _+2 potency_ rune, or 4d6 damage if the armor has a _+3 potency_ rune. However, whether you hit or miss, you take piercing damage equal to the number of d6s of piercing damage you would deal your attacker.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Twining Chains

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Skyrider Sword](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1432)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 106

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=24)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303) [greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=24)_ has a broad, flat blade that can support the weight of a Medium-sized or smaller wielder. Magic allows the weapon to soar through the air, carrying its wielder along with it.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You're holding the sword in at least one hand; **Effect** You leap upon your sword as it soars through the air for up to 10 minutes. You gain a fly Speed of 25 feet or your land Speed, whichever is slower, but you can't Strike with your weapon except via the weapon's other activation (see below). You can Dismiss the activation, and it ends automatically if you cease holding the weapon with at least one hand.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;    **Requirements** You are riding on your weapon; **Effect** You ride your weapon into a foe, attacking it as you fly past. You Fly, making a melee Strike with the weapon once at any point during your movement.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 19"

>
Skyrider Sword (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 106

**Price** 32,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** The sword is a _+3 greater striking greater shock greatsword_. You can activate the weapon's flight any number of times per day, and its duration is no longer limited to 10 minutes.
name: Skyrider Sword

---

When etched with this rune, a weapon's hilt or haft becomes engraved with grooves that match the imprints of a wolf's teeth. By putting a _fanged_ weapon in your mouth, you can transform into an animal.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157))    **Effect** You transform into a Small or Medium animal that wields the _fanged_ weapon in its jaws; the animal matches the animal you are most closely associated with (a [lizardfolk](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=15) would turn into a lizard, a [kitsune](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=38) into a fox, a [deer instinct](/Instincts.aspx?ID=1) [barbarian](/Classes.aspx?ID=2) into a deer, etc.) or a wolf if no specific animal is applicable.
