markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Brooch of Inspiration](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1434)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This finely cut garnet brooch fills your mind with vigor and occasional bursts of mental clarity. While wearing the brooch, you gain a +1 item bonus to checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) with [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skills.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You think hard on a topic and receive a sudden inspiration. You attempt to Recall Knowledge using Lore. On this check, you roll twice and take the higher result.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Brooch of Inspiration
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

**Price** 425 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Brooch of Inspiration (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, and the frequency for the activation is once per hour.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Brooch of Inspiration (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

**Price** 12,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3 and applies to all checks to Recall Knowledge with any skill. The activation has a frequency of once per 10 minutes, takes only a single action to activate, and you can attempt to Recall Knowledge with any skill.
name: Brooch of Inspiration

<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This finely cut garnet brooch fills your mind with vigor and occasional bursts of mental clarity. While wearing the brooch, you gain a +1 item bonus to checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) with [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skills.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You think hard on a topic and receive a sudden inspiration. You attempt to Recall Knowledge using Lore. On this check, you roll twice and take the higher result.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, and the frequency for the activation is once per hour.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Brooch of Inspiration (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

**Price** 12,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3 and applies to all checks to Recall Knowledge with any skill. The activation has a frequency of once per 10 minutes, takes only a single action to activate, and you can attempt to Recall Knowledge with any skill.
name: Brooch of Inspiration (Major)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crown of the Fire Eater](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1435)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A wreath of flames dances around the rim of this golden crown. You gain resistance 5 to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You take fire damage;  **Effect** You absorb some of the flame that would harm you. Increase your fire resistance from the crown from 5 to 15. Just after taking any remaining fire damage, you regain a number of Hit Points equal to 15 or the fire damage dealt by the triggering attack before damage resistance, whichever is less.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Crown of the Fire Eater
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Crown of the Fire Eater (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

**Price** 2,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The constant fire resistance is 10. When activated, the fire resistance and maximum number of Hit Points regained are each 25.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Crown of the Fire Eater (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

**Price** 10,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The constant fire resistance is 15. When activated, the fire resistance and maximum number of Hit Points regained are each 35.
name: Crown of the Fire Eater

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fleshgem](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1436)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** envision

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Fist](/Weapons.aspx?ID=356)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Originally developed as a body modification by [oreads](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=34), _fleshgems_ are crystals that can be implanted in the skin of a creature of any ancestry. While a _fleshgem_ can be applied anywhere on the body for cosmetic purposes, the most common usage among adventurers is to implant them at the base of the fingers, to be used like brass knuckles.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fleshgem (Combat)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

These pointed crystal shards embedded in your fingers grant you a [fist](/Weapons.aspx?ID=1) Strike that deals 1d6 piercing damage, is in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group, and has the [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) traits. _Combat fleshgems_ can be enhanced with the effects of [_handwraps of mighty blows_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441), as normal for an unarmed attack.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fleshgem (Earthspeaker)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

**Price** 450 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You siphon your fleshgem's power, and the earth around you erupts in crystal stalagmites.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action to Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You are standing on the ground; **Effect** The ground around you erupts in a 10-foot burst of knee-high crystal shards that remain for 1 round, or 1 minute if you spent two actions. To all creatures other than you, the area is difficult terrain as well as [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=454). Creatures that move through a space containing crystal shards take 2 piercing damage.
name: Fleshgem

These pointed crystal shards embedded in your fingers grant you a [fist](/Weapons.aspx?ID=1) Strike that deals 1d6 piercing damage, is in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group, and has the [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) traits. _Combat fleshgems_ can be enhanced with the effects of [_handwraps of mighty blows_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441), as normal for an unarmed attack.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fleshgem (Earthspeaker)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

**Price** 450 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You siphon your fleshgem's power, and the earth around you erupts in crystal stalagmites.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action to Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You are standing on the ground; **Effect** The ground around you erupts in a 10-foot burst of knee-high crystal shards that remain for 1 round, or 1 minute if you spent two actions. To all creatures other than you, the area is difficult terrain as well as [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=454). Creatures that move through a space containing crystal shards take 2 piercing damage.
name: Fleshgem (Earthspeaker)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lady's Knife](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1437)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)_ has an elaborate, gemmed handle and can be worn strapped to the inside of a wrist or tucked within a decorative bodice. Despite the name, which stems from the dagger's historical basis in Oppara as a lady's favor, this dagger is common among wielders of all genders and has become quite fashionable in Absalom of late, with most nobles who carry them matching their evening finery to the gems on the hilt.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You offer this weapon as a romantic or platonic favor to an ally. You and the ally become linked for 1 day. During this time, you each benefit from the effects of [_status_](/Spells.aspx?ID=308), using each other as the targets.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You have become linked with an ally by offering them the _lady's knife_; **Effect** You call forth the blade. The _lady's knife_ instantly teleports to your hand. The attempt to recall the knife fails if you are more than 1 mile away from the knife's current location. Your linked ally can use the same Activation to recall the knife once per day as well.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lady's Knife

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magnifying Glass of Elucidation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1438)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 110

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The magnifying glass of elucidation is available for purchase for any common languages, as well as any other languages to which a PC has gained access._

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These well-aged wooden magnifying glasses are engraved with a variety of runes. Each is imbued with a specific language. When you use a magnifying glass to examine writing of its imbued language, it translates the writing into a language you understand. For example, an elf who speaks only [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4) using a _magnifying glass of elucidation_ imbued with [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3) would see Dwarven writing as Elven when observed through the magnifying glass. The magnifying glass only provides direct translations and doesn't automatically allow you to understand codes or extremely esoteric passages—you still need to attempt a skill check to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22).

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You scan up to two pages of writing with the _magnifying glass of elucidation_, imbuing the magnifying glass with the information you scanned. You can use a separate 3-action activity to have the magnifying glass reproduce the imbued information onto blank paper exactly as it appeared when you Activated the magnifying glass. The reproduction is a direct copy of the pages but doesn't imbue the reproduction with any magical effects or other special effects. As such, you can copy the writing of a scroll, for example, but it will only be mundane writing and not have any magical effect. The magnifying glass can only hold one instance of information at a given time; Activating the magnifying glass a second time or reproducing the information clears out the magnifying glass's information.

**Craft Requirements** You know the language imbued in the magnifying glass.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Magnifying Glass of Elucidation

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mirror of Sleeping Vigil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1439)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Sleep" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=145" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 111

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This jeweled mirror grants you gain greater control over your dreaming self. While invested, you can watch over your sleeping body from the [Dreamlands](/Planes.aspx?ID=22), letting you see your own body and a 5-foot emanation around you, though you can't hear, smell, or use other senses from your dreaming self. You do not take the –4 status penalty to visual Perception checks in that area or gain the [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) condition from being [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) against creatures in that area. As long as you fell asleep voluntarily and not from a [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) effect, you can automatically wake up from sleep if there is visible activity around you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mirror of Sleeping Vigil

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Barricade Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1441)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Structure" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=153" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk**  when not activated
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>command, Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The ancient inhabitants of Bloodsalt crafted these magic rocks to construct defenses quickly. In its stone form, a _barricade stone_ appears to be a simple pebble with an unnaturally geometric shape. The size and shape of the stone's final form depends on the type of _barricade stone_. When you activate the barricade stone, you drop it on the ground, throw it up to 20 feet away, or sling it using a [sling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=13) weapon. Upon landing, the stone instantly and permanently expands. The stone grows to its maximum size. While normally items with the [structure](/Traits.aspx?ID=153) trait put creatures in their area inside the structure, this effect creates solid stone, so it can't be placed in an area where there are creatures. While it's likely to take some time even with the right tools, an activated _barricade stone_ can be destroyed the same as any normal stone structure (Hardness 14, HP 56).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Limited"
>
Barricade Stone (Sphere)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 75

**Price** 40 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This smooth marble keeps its shape when activated, growing into a spherical boulder 10 feet in diameter. If stationary, the boulder can be pushed 5 feet with a successful DC 24 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check. If the marble was thrown or slung, the boulder retains its momentum, moving 25 feet away from you each round. A creature in the boulder's way takes 4d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save; on a failure, the creature is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31)).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Limited"
>
Barricade Stone (Cube)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 75

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This seemingly simple six-sided die was designed to block a corridor. When activated, it grows to fill a solid square or rectangular space no more than 20 feet on any side. The cube fills spaces well enough that Tiny creatures can't slip through, but it's far from a perfect seal. Liquids, gases, and minuscule creatures such as insects can still find ways around it. Depending on the irregularities of the space the cube fills, it might be possible to see or hear past it.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Limited"
>
Barricade Stone (Cylinder)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 75

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, this cylinder expands to a diameter of 5 feet, 10 feet, or 20 feet, depending on the command, with respective maximum heights of 80 feet, 40 feet, and 20 feet. If a dimension can't be reached due to contact with another solid surface, the cylinder ceases to grow in that direction. You can activate the cylinder horizontally instead of vertically. If you do, the cylinder moves similarly to the sphere barricade stone, with the following changes: both DCs increase to 27, the damage increases to 6d6, and a creature that critically fails its Reflex save against the rolling cylinder gains the [wounded 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) condition (or increases the value of its wounded condition by 1).
name: Barricade Stone

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Barricade Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1441)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Structure" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=153" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk**  when not activated
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>command, Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The ancient inhabitants of Bloodsalt crafted these magic rocks to construct defenses quickly. In its stone form, a _barricade stone_ appears to be a simple pebble with an unnaturally geometric shape. The size and shape of the stone's final form depends on the type of _barricade stone_. When you activate the barricade stone, you drop it on the ground, throw it up to 20 feet away, or sling it using a [sling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=13) weapon. Upon landing, the stone instantly and permanently expands. The stone grows to its maximum size. While normally items with the [structure](/Traits.aspx?ID=153) trait put creatures in their area inside the structure, this effect creates solid stone, so it can't be placed in an area where there are creatures. While it's likely to take some time even with the right tools, an activated _barricade stone_ can be destroyed the same as any normal stone structure (Hardness 14, HP 56).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Limited"
>
Barricade Stone (Sphere)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 75

**Price** 40 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This smooth marble keeps its shape when activated, growing into a spherical boulder 10 feet in diameter. If stationary, the boulder can be pushed 5 feet with a successful DC 24 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check. If the marble was thrown or slung, the boulder retains its momentum, moving 25 feet away from you each round. A creature in the boulder's way takes 4d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save; on a failure, the creature is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31)).

level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Limited"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 80 gp

</row>

This seemingly simple six-sided die was designed to block a corridor. When activated, it grows to fill a solid square or rectangular space no more than 20 feet on any side. The cube fills spaces well enough that Tiny creatures can't slip through, but it's far from a perfect seal. Liquids, gases, and minuscule creatures such as insects can still find ways around it. Depending on the irregularities of the space the cube fills, it might be possible to see or hear past it.

When activated, this cylinder expands to a diameter of 5 feet, 10 feet, or 20 feet, depending on the command, with respective maximum heights of 80 feet, 40 feet, and 20 feet. If a dimension can't be reached due to contact with another solid surface, the cylinder ceases to grow in that direction. You can activate the cylinder horizontally instead of vertically. If you do, the cylinder moves similarly to the sphere barricade stone, with the following changes: both DCs increase to 27, the damage increases to 6d6, and a creature that critically fails its Reflex save against the rolling cylinder gains the [wounded 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) condition (or increases the value of its wounded condition by 1).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Dragon Rune Bracelet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1442)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 75, [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 148

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn bracelet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This polished golden bracelet is formed around the scale of a famous dragon. The bracelet is etched with esoteric symbols or words—always in [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2)—that indicate kinship with dragons. As many types of _dragon rune bracelets_ exist as there are types of dragons, though dragon rune bracelets associated with [Uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=159) or [Rare](/Traits.aspx?ID=137) dragons are Rare.

 While wearing a dragon rune bracelet, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks while interacting with dragons of the same type as the pendant's scale (such as [red dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=168) or [cloud dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=176)). You also gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws against [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) effects from any kind of [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50).

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You can cast bloodline spells from the [draconic bloodline](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=5), such as from the [sorcerer](/Classes.aspx?ID=11) class or [sorcerer multiclass archetype](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=11); **Effect** You regain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a sorcerer bloodline spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of your turn, it is lost.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragon Rune Bracelet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Exploration Lens](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1443)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _exploration lens_ is a magical glass lens designed for a [bull's-eye lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=29). When light from the lantern passes through the lens, it alters the light to make it easier to perform a common exploration activity with the lantern. You can find more information about exploration activities related to these lenses on page 496 of the Core Rulebook. Alternatively, an _exploration lens_ can be installed in a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470) like an [_aeon stone_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407). In this case, light (such as that from a lantern, [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60), or [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171) spell) must still pass through the lens and you must hold the _wayfinder_ in your hand for it to function.

 There are several different types of exploration lens, each granting benefits with a different type of exploration activity. Each type of lens is distinguishable from all the other types by holding it up to a bright light.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Limited"
>
Exploration Lens (Detecting)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When held up to light, this lens sparkles as though infused with glitter. While [Detecting Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=513) in exploration mode, when the lantern's light falls upon something you detect as magical, it pulsates faintly. This makes it easier to triangulate the magic, since you can use the cone of the bull's-eye lantern instead of the circular pulse of [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66). You also gain a +2 item bonus to checks to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24) within the light.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Limited"
>
Exploration Lens (Investigating)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The outer rim of this lens glows faintly when held up to the light. While activated, the lens grants you a +1 item bonus to skill checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) while [Investigating](/Actions.aspx?ID=516) by outlining potential clues in a way that triggers your memory. If you're Investigating something while using the lens, you can always roll a Recall Knowledge skill check appropriate to the investigation for initiative.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Limited"
>
Exploration Lens (Searching)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This lens has an opalescent sheen when held up to light. The lens subtly outlines hidden doors and concealed hazards, granting you a +2 item bonus to Perception checks to spot such features while [Searching](/Actions.aspx?ID=519). Even if you aren't Searching, you get a check to find [traps](/Traits.aspx?ID=158) that normally require you to Search.
name: Exploration Lens

</traits>

<additional-info>
**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Exploration Lens](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1443)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _exploration lens_ is a magical glass lens designed for a [bull's-eye lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=29). When light from the lantern passes through the lens, it alters the light to make it easier to perform a common exploration activity with the lantern. You can find more information about exploration activities related to these lenses on page 496 of the Core Rulebook. Alternatively, an _exploration lens_ can be installed in a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470) like an [_aeon stone_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407). In this case, light (such as that from a lantern, [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60), or [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171) spell) must still pass through the lens and you must hold the _wayfinder_ in your hand for it to function.

 There are several different types of exploration lens, each granting benefits with a different type of exploration activity. Each type of lens is distinguishable from all the other types by holding it up to a bright light.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Limited"
>
Exploration Lens (Detecting)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When held up to light, this lens sparkles as though infused with glitter. While [Detecting Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=513) in exploration mode, when the lantern's light falls upon something you detect as magical, it pulsates faintly. This makes it easier to triangulate the magic, since you can use the cone of the bull's-eye lantern instead of the circular pulse of [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66). You also gain a +2 item bonus to checks to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24) within the light.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Limited"
>
Exploration Lens (Investigating)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The outer rim of this lens glows faintly when held up to the light. While activated, the lens grants you a +1 item bonus to skill checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) while [Investigating](/Actions.aspx?ID=516) by outlining potential clues in a way that triggers your memory. If you're Investigating something while using the lens, you can always roll a Recall Knowledge skill check appropriate to the investigation for initiative.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Limited"
>
Exploration Lens (Searching)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This lens has an opalescent sheen when held up to light. The lens subtly outlines hidden doors and concealed hazards, granting you a +2 item bonus to Perception checks to spot such features while [Searching](/Actions.aspx?ID=519). Even if you aren't Searching, you get a check to find [traps](/Traits.aspx?ID=158) that normally require you to Search.
name: Exploration Lens (Detecting)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Ixamè's Eye](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1446)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to headgear

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Via some strange alchemical reaction with the gasses of Terwa Lake, the [cloud dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=176) Ixamè's eyeballs have shriveled into sky-blue gems. The dragon's sense of vision isn't impaired, and her eyes can't be removed from her head until she's destroyed. Once removed, each gem functions as a special talisman. _Ixamè's eye_ sparkles when it's in an area of fog or mist. Activating the talisman enables you to see clearly through fog and mist for 1 minute; any creature [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) only by fog or mist is no longer concealed to you. If such a creature was relying on the concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63), this also means it's no longer [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ixamè's Eye

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Red-Rib Gill Mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1447)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This mask is made from the gills of the red-rib salamander, an amphibious creature unique to Terwa Lake. Once activated, the mask intensifies the odors of gaseous toxins, allowing you to automatically detect toxic fumes within 30 feet and their approximate source (making the source [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) instead of [unnoticed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=41)). You can't wear other masks while you're wearing a _red-rib gill mask_.

 After Activating the mask, you also gain the listed item bonus to Fortitude saves against [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96) effects; this bonus is increased against the deadly volcanic gas known around Terwa Lake as Dead Man's Breath, and possibly against other similar volcanic gases at the GM's discretion.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Limited"
>
Red-Rib Gill Mask (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

**Price** 7 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +1, or +2 against Dead Man's Breath, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Limited"
>
Red-Rib Gill Mask (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, or +3 against Dead Man's Breath, and the duration is 1 hour.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Limited"
>
Red-Rib Gill Mask (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3, or +4 against Dead Man's Breath, and the duration is 8 hours.
name: Red-Rib Gill Mask

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Red-Rib Gill Mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1447)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This mask is made from the gills of the red-rib salamander, an amphibious creature unique to Terwa Lake. Once activated, the mask intensifies the odors of gaseous toxins, allowing you to automatically detect toxic fumes within 30 feet and their approximate source (making the source [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) instead of [unnoticed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=41)). You can't wear other masks while you're wearing a _red-rib gill mask_.

 After Activating the mask, you also gain the listed item bonus to Fortitude saves against [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96) effects; this bonus is increased against the deadly volcanic gas known around Terwa Lake as Dead Man's Breath, and possibly against other similar volcanic gases at the GM's discretion.
